channel.title: Realistic Cases on GOTM - General Ocean Turbulence Model
channel.description: Recent content in Realistic Cases on GOTM - General Ocean Turbulence Model

item.title: Blacksea
item.description:
Blacksea#

Black Sea - Permanent stratification, anoxic conditions

Overview#

Repository: cases/blacksea

Running the Case#

cd cases/blacksea
gotm

Output: result.nc

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Validation#

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

ds = nc.Dataset('result.nc')
temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram
plt.contourf(temp.T, levels=20)
plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')
plt.ylabel('Depth (m)')
plt.xlabel('Time')
plt.show()

References#

See the publications page for papers using this case.

item.title: Flex
item.description:
Flex#

FLEX 1976 - Fladen Ground Experiment, historic North Sea measurements

Overview#

Repository: cases/flex

Running the Case#

cd cases/flex
gotm

Output: result.nc

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Validation#

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

ds = nc.Dataset('result.nc')
temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram
plt.contourf(temp.T, levels=20)
plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')
plt.ylabel('Depth (m)')
plt.xlabel('Time')
plt.show()

References#

See the publications page for papers using this case.

item.title: Gotland
item.description:
Gotland#

Gotland Deep - Baltic Sea seasonal stratification dynamics

Overview#

Repository: cases/gotland

Running the Case#

cd cases/gotland
gotm

Output: result.nc

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Validation#

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc

ds = nc.Dataset('result.nc')
temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram
plt.contourf(temp.T, levels=20)
plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')
plt.ylabel('Depth (m)')
plt.xlabel('Time')
plt.show()

item.title: Lago Maggiore
item.description:
Lago Maggiore#

Lake Maggiore - Italian lake with stratification and seasonal overturn

Overview#

Repository: cases/lago_maggiore

Running the Case#

cd cases/lago_maggiore
gotm

Output: result.nc

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Validation#

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

ds = nc.Dataset('result.nc')
temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram
plt.contourf(temp.T, levels=20)
plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')
plt.ylabel('Depth (m)')
plt.xlabel('Time')
plt.show()

See the publications page for papers using this case.

item.title: Liverpool Bay
item.description:
Liverpool Bay#

Liverpool Bay - Tidal mixing and ROFI dynamics in Irish Sea

Overview#

Repository: cases/liverpool_bay

Running the Case#

cd cases/liverpool_bay
gotm

Output: result.nc

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Validation#

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

ds = nc.Dataset('result.nc')
temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram
plt.contourf(temp.T, levels=20)
plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')
plt.ylabel('Depth (m)')
plt.xlabel('Time')
plt.show()

item.title: Medsea East
item.description:
Medsea East#

Eastern Mediterranean - Oligotrophic conditions, deep convection

Overview#

Repository: cases/medsea_east

Running the Case#

cd cases/medsea_east
gotm

Output: result.nc

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Validation#

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

ds = nc.Dataset('result.nc')
temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram
plt.contourf(temp.T, levels=20)
plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')
plt.ylabel('Depth (m)')
plt.xlabel('Time')
plt.show()

item.title: Medsea West

item.title: Nns Annual
item.description:
Nns Annual#

Northern North Sea annual - Full seasonal cycle, temperate shelf

Overview#

Repository: cases/nns_annual

Running the Case#

cd cases/nns_annual
gotm

Output: result.nc

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Validation#

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc

ds = nc.Dataset('result.nc')
temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram

plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')
plt.ylabel('Depth (m)')
plt.xlabel('Time')

item.title: Nns Seasonal
item.description:
Nns Seasonal#

Repository: cases/nns_seasonal

cd cases/nns_seasonal

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc

temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram

plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')

item.title: Ows Papa
item.description:

z = ds.variables['z'][:]

item.title: Resolute
item.description:
Resolute#

Arctic Resolute Passage - Sea ice and under-ice mixing

Overview#

Repository: cases/resolute

Running the Case#

cd cases/resolute
gotm

Output: result.nc

Configuration#

This case uses realistic forcing data. See files in the case directory:

  • gotm.yaml - Main configuration
  • *_file.dat - Forcing data files

Validation#

Compare results with observations or climatology where available.

Analysis Tips#

import netCDF4 as nc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

ds = nc.Dataset('result.nc')
temp = ds.variables['temp'][:]
z = ds.variables['z'][:]

# Hovmoller diagram
plt.contourf(temp.T, levels=20)
plt.colorbar(label='Temperature (°C)')
plt.ylabel('Depth (m)')
plt.xlabel('Time')
plt.show()

See the publications page for papers using this case.
